unit.note: Server vitest 164 files / 1915 passed + 1 skipped (1916) green (F0 gate, SHIPWRIGHT_NETWORK_PROFILE=local); server tsc --noEmit clean + build green. D09 is server-only (server/src/external/file/write.ts, new server/src/external/file/__tests__/write-symlink-guard.test.ts) so the client suite (unchanged since D07 - no D09 client files) was not re-run. New F09 coverage: write-symlink-guard.test.ts (3 passed + 1 skipped: a `.md`-named symlink target is rejected 403 symlink_forbidden with the escape target byte-identical - via a real symlink through createExternalRoutes (skipped on this Windows host, EPERM: file symlinks need admin/Developer Mode; RUNS on Linux CI ubuntu-latest) AND via the injected lstat seam (deterministic, cross-platform); a symlink reported non-file (broken link / symlink-to-dir) is still 403; a regular .md write still succeeds).

integration.note: Confidence-calibration probes of the touches_io_boundary write surface (Windows 11; file symlinks unavailable - symlinkSync returns EPERM without Developer Mode/admin - so the boundary is probed through the registerMarkdownWrite lstat seam, and the real-symlink end-to-end path is exercised on Linux CI). (1) Rejection probe: the route rejects a target whose lstat reports isSymbolicLink() (both symlink-to-file and non-file/broken-symlink) with 403 symlink_forbidden BEFORE the If-Match/read/write - the on-disk stand-in target is byte-identical. (2) RED-first sensitivity probe: with only the `if (lst.isSymbolicLink())` rejection removed, the SAME request writes through (200, target clobbered) - proving the probe detects the defect, not a false-green. Round-trip stability (GET ETag -> PUT If-Match -> GET ETag stable) for a regular .md write is confirmed by file-write-route.test.ts. Two consecutive clean rejection probes (symlink-to-file, non-file symlink) => asymptote reached.

e2e.note: No Playwright run. D09 is a server-only hardening of the PUT /api/external/projects/:id/file markdown-write route (FR-01.34) with NO UI-consumed contract change: no route shape (only a new 403 symlink_forbidden rejection branch), no DOM/prop/render, no Vite-config/proxy/WS-origin/CORS surface changed. detect_frontend_changes.py reports has_frontend_changes=false. A browser smoke adds no signal the deterministic server Vitest already provides. F0.5 surface=none.

surface_verification.justification: Server-only path-guard hardening of the markdown-write route (FR-01.34, F09). No UI-consumed contract changed: no /api route shape (adds only a 403 symlink_forbidden branch), no DOM/prop/render, no Vite-config/proxy/WS-origin/CORS surface; detect_frontend_changes.py = has_frontend_changes:false. AC2 correctness is proven by a RED-first server test: on pre-fix code a within-root `.md`-named symlink write SUCCEEDS (200, target clobbered) - captured on this Windows host via the injected lstat seam with the rejection line removed, and end-to-end via a real `notes.md -> config.json` symlink on Linux CI (ubuntu-latest); GREEN after (403 symlink_forbidden, escape target byte-identical). A browser run would add no signal the deterministic unit tests lack, so F0.5 surface=none (no fabricated browser run).

behaviors.behavior: AC1 - PUT /file rejects a within-root .md-named symlink escape: the markdown-only write boundary previously validated the .md/.markdown allowlist on the pathGuard string-resolved path (the symlink NAME) while the write followed the link to its realpath, and realPathGuard only re-checked CONTAINMENT - so a `notes.md -> shipwright_run_config.json` (or -> package.json / a build script) passed both checks and clobbered a non-markdown file. The fix lstat's the target (does not follow the final component) and rejects any final-component symlink with 403 symlink_forbidden; the read+rename write target is now the requested path (not the realpath) so renameSync replaces the final directory entry and cannot dereference a TOCTOU-swapped symlink. realPathGuard is retained as defense-in-depth for a parent-directory symlink escape.
behaviors.evidence: server/src/external/file/__tests__/write-symlink-guard.test.ts - real symlink `notes.md -> config.json` through createExternalRoutes returns 403 symlink_forbidden and leaves config.json byte-identical (runs on Linux CI; skipped where file symlinks need elevation); injected-lstat seam rejects a symlink-to-file target 403 with no write-through; a symlink reported non-file (broken link / symlink-to-dir) is still 403; a regular .md write still succeeds (guard is symlink-specific). Existing file-write-route.test.ts (415 non-md, 400 traversal, 409 stale If-Match, atomic round-trip) stays green.

behaviors.behavior: AC2 - a NEW regression proof is RED on pre-fix main and GREEN after
behaviors.evidence: RED captured on this Windows host: with only the `if (lst.isSymbolicLink())` rejection removed (pre-fix behavior), the mocked-lstat test FAILS - expected 403, got 200; the PUT wrote through to the target (write SUCCEEDED, defect reproduced). GREEN after the fix (403 symlink_forbidden, target byte-identical). On Linux CI the real-symlink test reproduces the same RED->green end-to-end (a `.md`-named symlink write escaping the markdown-only boundary succeeds pre-fix, rejected after).

behaviors.behavior: AC3 - full server suite + build stay green (no invariant/ADR regressed)
behaviors.evidence: F0: server vitest 164 files / 1915 passed + 1 skipped (1916) (SHIPWRIGHT_NETWORK_PROFILE=local); server tsc --noEmit clean + build green. path-guard.test.ts, file-route.test.ts, file-write-route.test.ts, file/__tests__/routes.test.ts all green. CLAUDE.md rule 10 (realpath+path.relative containment, NOT startsWith) intact + strengthened; read-only-observer rules 1/12 intact (no writes to ~/.claude/projects/** or shipwright_run_config.json). Client untouched (server-only iterate).

behaviors.behavior: AC4 - files stay within the expected footprint; bloat baseline not ratcheted
behaviors.evidence: Touched write.ts (186 -> 225 < 300, not baselined) and one NEW non-baselined test file write-symlink-guard.test.ts (246 < 300). New test file (cohesive symlink-escape defense) is used instead of the spec-listed file-route.test.ts because that file is bloat-baselined at 312 (grandfathered, limit 300) - adding to it would RATCHET the baseline (anti-ratchet contract violation); the campaign guardrail sanctions a new cohesive *.test.ts. shipwright_bloat_baseline.json not modified (no ratchet; anti-ratchet hook clean). D09 is serial #9 with all predecessors merged, so the parallel-safety footprint contract has zero collision risk.
